free_text: A thin B-nat target and an enriched B-10 one were used.
Both targets were prepared by electron gun evaporation
on thick tantalum backings.In addition,two pellets for
benchmarking purposes were prepared by pressing
amorphous powder, a B-nat and a MgB(2) one. As boron is
an insulator, an ultra thin layer of Au was evaporated
on its surface, as to avoid electrical sparks during
the irradiations. The thin B-10 enriched target was
characterized by applying the Elastic Backscattering
Spectrometry (EBS) method. The thicknesses of the
enriched B-10 and of the B-nat targets were found to be
a 1000 mum thick surface barrier detector (SSB)
Statistical uncertainty due to peak integration
ERR-T -> ERR-S. Data at 0. degr. added.
